evals.prompt: Create a spec for our new webhook-delivery service. It will expose REST endpoints for registering webhook URLs, listing subscriptions, and triggering test deliveries. The service reads events from a Kafka topic and fans out HTTP POST requests to registered endpoints with retries. We're building this in Go with a PostgreSQL database for subscription storage.
evals.expected_output: Creates paired spec.md and design.md under docs/openspec/specs/webhook-delivery/. The spec includes a Security Requirements section (web-facing: REST endpoints over HTTP), auth-by-default endpoint table with all endpoints defaulting to Auth: Required, and backend quality guidelines for error handling (service boundaries, external HTTP calls), concurrency (fan-out workers, Kafka consumer), and database operations (PostgreSQL). No Accessibility Requirements section (API-only, no UI).

assertions.text: spec.md created at docs/openspec/specs/webhook-delivery/spec.md
design.md created at docs/openspec/specs/webhook-delivery/design.md (paired artifact)
spec.md contains a '## Security Requirements' section with all six subsections (Authentication, Rate Limiting, Security Headers, Request Body Size Limits, CSRF Protection, Redirect Validation)
Endpoint table uses auth-by-default: all endpoints show 'Auth: Required' unless explicitly marked 'Auth: Public' with justification
spec.md contains '### Requirement: Error Handling Standards' with error wrapping, sentinel errors, no silent swallowing, and structured logging
spec.md contains '### Requirement: Concurrency Safety' with context propagation, worker lifecycle, race safety, and race detection CI
spec.md contains '### Requirement: Database Operation Standards' with transactions, connection lifecycle, and parameterized queries
spec.md does NOT contain an '## Accessibility Requirements' section (API-only, no UI)
spec.md uses RFC 2119 keywords (MUST, SHALL, SHOULD, MAY, etc.) for all normative requirements
spec.md uses SPEC-XXXX numbering (not RFC-XXXX)
design.md contains at least one Mermaid diagram
Backend quality guidelines use language-agnostic terms (not Go-specific like 'goroutines' or 'slog')

evals.prompt: We need a spec for an admin dashboard. It's a web UI built with Go html/template and HTMX that lets ops staff manage feature flags, view system health, and browse audit logs. It has server-rendered pages with modals for creating/editing flags, a real-time status panel that auto-refreshes, and REST endpoints backing the UI. The backend uses PostgreSQL.
evals.expected_output: Creates paired spec.md and design.md. The spec is both web-facing (REST endpoints, HTTP server) AND UI-facing (html/template, HTMX, modals, dashboard). It includes BOTH Security Requirements and Accessibility Requirements sections, plus auth-by-default endpoint tables and backend quality guidelines for error handling and database operations.

assertions.text: spec.md created at docs/openspec/specs/admin-dashboard/spec.md
design.md created at docs/openspec/specs/admin-dashboard/design.md (paired artifact)
spec.md contains a '## Security Requirements' section (web-facing: REST endpoints)
spec.md contains an '## Accessibility Requirements' section (UI-facing: html/template, HTMX, modals)
Accessibility section includes WCAG 2.1 AA, ARIA landmarks, aria-label for icon-only controls, aria-live for dynamic content (auto-refresh panel), keyboard navigation, and focus management (modals)
Endpoint table uses auth-by-default: all endpoints default to 'Auth: Required'
spec.md contains backend quality requirements for error handling and database operations
spec.md uses RFC 2119 keywords for all normative requirements
Scenarios use exactly #### heading level (not ### or bullets)
Every requirement has at least one scenario with WHEN/THEN format

evals.prompt: Create a spec for a CLI tool called db-migrate that runs database schema migrations. It reads migration files from a directory, connects to PostgreSQL, applies them in order inside transactions, and logs results. It's a command-line tool only -- no web server, no UI, no HTTP endpoints. It does handle concurrent migration locking to prevent two instances from running at once.
evals.expected_output: Creates paired spec.md and design.md. This is a CLI tool with NO web or UI component. It MUST NOT include Security Requirements or Accessibility Requirements sections. It SHOULD include backend quality guidelines for error handling (database operations, file I/O), concurrency (migration locking), and database operations (transactions, connections, parameterized queries).

assertions.text: spec.md created at docs/openspec/specs/db-migrate/spec.md
design.md created at docs/openspec/specs/db-migrate/design.md (paired artifact)
spec.md does NOT contain a '## Security Requirements' section (CLI tool, not web-facing)
spec.md does NOT contain an '## Accessibility Requirements' section (CLI tool, not UI-facing)
spec.md contains '### Requirement: Error Handling Standards' (database operations, file I/O can fail)
spec.md contains '### Requirement: Concurrency Safety' (concurrent migration locking)
spec.md contains '### Requirement: Database Operation Standards' (transactions, connections, parameterized queries)
spec.md uses SPEC-XXXX numbering (not RFC-XXXX)
design.md contains at least one Mermaid diagram

evals.prompt: Check src/api/ for drift against our ADRs and specs
evals.expected_output: Performs a drift check on the src/api/ directory. Scans for security lint patterns (unbounded body read, template safety bypass, user-controlled redirect, missing auth middleware, DOM injection, CDN without SRI) in source files. Produces a findings table with Severity, Category, Finding, Source, and Location columns. Security lint findings use category 'Security Lint' and severity [WARNING].

assertions.text: Output contains '## Drift Check: src/api/' heading
Output contains a findings table with columns: Severity, Category, Finding, Source, Location
Security lint findings (if any) use category 'Security Lint' and severity '[WARNING]'
Security lint scan checks for all 6 patterns: unbounded body read, template safety bypass, user-controlled redirect, missing auth middleware, DOM injection, CDN without SRI
Each security lint finding includes file path with line number in Location column
Each security lint finding includes a remediation suggestion
Output ends with a Summary section showing Critical/Warning/Info counts
Output includes suggested actions based on findings

evals.prompt: Run a drift check on our templates/ directory
evals.expected_output: Performs a drift check on the templates/ directory. Scans for template quality patterns: duplicate inline script blocks, duplicate form structures, duplicate navigation/sidebar markup, dev-only CDN URLs, and multiple JS interaction frameworks. Template quality findings use category 'Template Quality' with individually defined severities ([WARNING] for duplicate scripts/nav/dev CDN, [INFO] for duplicate forms/framework sprawl).

assertions.text: Output contains '## Drift Check: templates/' heading
Template quality scan checks for all 5 patterns: duplicate inline scripts, duplicate form structure, duplicate navigation/sidebar, dev-only CDN URLs, multiple JS frameworks
Template quality findings use category 'Template Quality'
Duplicate inline script findings use severity '[WARNING]'
Duplicate form structure findings use severity '[INFO]'
Dev-only CDN findings use severity '[WARNING]'
Multiple JS framework findings use severity '[INFO]'
Each template quality finding includes affected file paths in Location column

evals.prompt: Check this project for drift. There are no ADRs or specs yet.
evals.expected_output: Reports that no design artifacts were found and suggests creating them. Does NOT proceed with analysis. Suggests using /design:adr or /design:spec to create first artifacts.

assertions.text: Output reports 'No design artifacts found' (or equivalent message when neither ADR nor spec directories exist)
Output suggests '/design:adr' to create first ADR
Output suggests '/design:spec' to create first spec
Does NOT produce a findings table (no artifacts to check against)

evals.prompt: Discover what implicit architecture decisions exist in this codebase. We haven't documented anything yet.
evals.expected_output: Produces a discovery report analyzing the entire project. Spawns 4 parallel agents for: dependency/framework analysis, architectural pattern analysis, project structure/boundary analysis, and configuration/infrastructure analysis. Report includes suggested ADRs (implicit decisions) and suggested specs (subsystem boundaries), each with confidence levels and copy-paste-ready commands. Does NOT create any files.

assertions.text: Output contains '## Discovery Report' heading
Output contains a '### Suggested ADRs' section with a table including Confidence, Decision, Evidence, and Command columns
Output contains a '### Suggested Specs' section with a table including Confidence, Subsystem, Boundary, and Command columns
Every suggestion includes a confidence level (High, Medium, or Low)
Every ADR suggestion includes a copy-paste-ready '/design:adr' command in the Command column
Every spec suggestion includes a copy-paste-ready '/design:spec' command in the Command column
Suggestions are sorted by confidence (High first, then Medium, then Low) within each section
Every suggestion cites specific evidence from the codebase (file paths, dependency declarations, config entries)
Output contains a '### Next Steps' section
No files are created or modified (read-only skill)

evals.prompt: Discover architecture in the src/payments/ directory
evals.expected_output: If src/payments/ does not exist, reports the error and stops without falling back to full-project analysis. If it does exist, scopes the discovery to that directory only.

assertions.text: If scope path does not exist, output contains 'Scope not found: src/payments/' error message
If scope path does not exist, does NOT fall back to full-project analysis
If scope path exists, output contains '## Discovery Report' scoped to src/payments/
No files are created or modified (read-only skill)

evals.prompt: Generate a documentation site for our project. We have ADRs in docs/adrs/ and specs in docs/openspec/specs/.
evals.expected_output: Runs pre-flight checks (Node.js, ADR/spec existence), detects no existing Docusaurus site, proceeds with scaffold mode. Creates docs-site/ directory from templates/docusaurus/, customizes package.json and docusaurus.config.ts, runs npm install, creates .design-docs.json manifest with tracked files and SHA-256 checksums, and updates .claudeignore.

assertions.text: Pre-flight check verifies Node.js is installed
Pre-flight check verifies ADR and/or spec directories have content
docs-site/ directory created from templates/docusaurus/ via cp -r
docs-site/package.json customized with project name
docs-site/docusaurus.config.ts customized with project title and URLs
npm install run in docs-site/ directory
.design-docs.json manifest created at project root with version, mode ('scaffold'), siteDir, createdAt, updatedAt, and files object
Manifest files object contains SHA-256 checksums in 'sha256:<hex-digest>' format
.claudeignore updated with docs-site/node_modules/, docs-site/build/, docs-site/.docusaurus/ entries
Offers to start the dev server after completion

evals.prompt: Generate docs for our project. We just initialized it yesterday -- no ADRs or specs yet.
evals.expected_output: Pre-flight check detects no ADR or spec content. Reports that no artifacts were found and suggests creating some with /design:adr or /design:spec. Does NOT proceed with site generation.

assertions.text: Output reports no ADRs or specs found
Output suggests '/design:adr' or '/design:spec' to create first artifacts
Does NOT create a docs-site/ directory
Does NOT create .design-docs.json
Does NOT run npm install
